required_inputs: request intent
store replenishment specialty boundary
spend, supplier, pricing, refund, food-safety, fraud, and approval authority map
adjacent specialist registry
refusal_triggers: request asks for spend approval, supplier selection, supplier-risk acceptance, pricing override, refund exception, food-safety release, fraud finding, or legal interpretation
request asks the lane to release recalled, stop-sale, held, expired, or unsafe product into saleable inventory
request asks the lane to mark replenishment complete without evidence

required_inputs: authoritative CIPS, NIST, FTC, FDA, FSIS, GS1, and GAO source set
source freshness indicators
tenant, supplier, and jurisdiction policy precedence
refusal_triggers: required public source context is missing or stale
jurisdictional, supplier, franchise, or tenant policy materially changes the recommendation
public guidance and tenant policy materially conflict

required_inputs: SKU, UPC, GTIN, PLU, item master, vendor item, pack, lot, and case records
POS movement, perpetual inventory, shelf audit, backroom count, cooler and freezer records
purchase order, store order, warehouse, DSD, ASN, in-transit, and fill-rate records
OMS, CRM, service desk, refund, recall, stop-sale, and loss-prevention artifacts
refusal_triggers: core item, inventory, vendor, order, customer, recall, hold, or safety records are absent or contradictory
SKU, UPC, GTIN, PLU, lot, or item-master evidence cannot be tied to requested replenishment work
availability restoration or completion evidence is missing

required_inputs: systems-of-record availability
tenant retrieval hooks
store, product, vendor, order, customer, service-level, tender, and jurisdiction constraints
approval and owner map
refusal_triggers: required POS, OMS, inventory, item master, vendor, warehouse, DSD, recall, CRM, service-desk, or pricing systems are unavailable or stale
required tenant policy or authority mapping is missing
next owner or prerequisite dependency is unknown

required_inputs: missing-fact indicators
confidence rubric
source, system, vendor, safety, customer-impact, fraud, or policy conflict signals
refusal_triggers: critical prerequisite remains unknown after retrieval attempts
source-of-truth conflict remains unresolved
policy, jurisdiction, supplier, order, recall, or authority conflict changes the safe answer

required_inputs: scope classification
adjacent specialist registry
handoff requirements
evidence packet
refusal_triggers: request belongs to pricing, promotions, customer service, supplier risk, procurement approval, food safety, fraud or loss prevention, logistics execution, legal, or executive operations ownership
request requires formal approval outside the lane
the lane cannot safely remediate the issue directly

required_inputs: on-hand, on-order, in-transit, backroom, shelf, and capacity state
POS movement and demand signals
warehouse, DSD, vendor, allocation, and replenishment calendar records
customer order and availability status when relevant
refusal_triggers: inventory or supply state cannot be tied to authoritative records
requested availability status ignores a hold, recall, vendor block, or missing item identity
customer-facing availability would be overstated

required_inputs: store order or purchase order state
vendor delivery and ASN records
approved buying channel and emergency-buy thresholds
supplier, warehouse, DSD, MOQ, order multiple, lead-time, and allocation constraints
refusal_triggers: requested vendor or procurement action requires spend approval, supplier approval, supplier risk acceptance, contract decision, or quality release
supplier, warehouse, or DSD evidence is missing or conflicting
the requested action would bypass approved buying channels

required_inputs: shortage, cut-order, allocation, or hold state
recall, stop-sale, food-safety, loss-prevention, or vendor hold records
customer-impact, promotion, and service-level context
authorized escalation path
required_outputs: owner routing and required approval list
refusal_triggers: shortage or hold would be hidden or closed without evidence
product is recalled, stop-sale, expired, unsafe, or identity-ambiguous
exception requires pricing, refund, supplier, quality, safety, fraud, legal, or financial approval

required_inputs: queue volume indicators
store, department, channel, and service mix
priority and SLA rules
customer order and service impact signals
refusal_triggers: volume pressure would skip inventory, order, vendor, hold, or traceability verification
customer workflow cannot be prioritized safely without prerequisites
queue response would imply refund, pricing, or service-recovery authority

required_inputs: customer-impact signal
refund, pricing, substitution, safety, fraud, and service policy limits
order, payment, CRM, service desk, recall, and loss-prevention evidence
refusal_triggers: customer harm, fraud, recall, safety, refund, pricing, payment, or policy exception determines the answer
request asks for policy exception approval outside delegated authority
customer-impact evidence is missing or contradictory

coverage_notes: All shared abilities, all supply-procurement-and-availability abilities, and all retail-consumer-and-hospitality abilities are declared and mapped to passing scenarios.
Every scenario in evaluation/results.json is represented in scenario_coverage.
